summary: Soundboard recording of a top‑tier ’72 show with sparkling performances of Mexicali Blues, Dark Star and more, though most comments focus on its commercial‑release inaccessibility.
review: The few musical reviews praise this May 1972 performance as a ‘top‑tier’ set, highlighting bright renditions of Mexicali Blues, Good Lovin', Dark Star, Morning Dew, Sugar Magnolia, Comes A Time and Uncle John's Band, with Jerry Garcia described as “primed”. Technically the tape appears to be a soundboard source that is speed‑corrected and clear, but most reviewers are preoccupied with the fact that the recording is only available commercially.

ai_rating.rationale: Positive comments about song quality and a likely good soundboard source are offset by limited substantive feedback and many reviews focused on access issues, yielding an overall moderately high rating with medium confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Version appears speed‑corrected and clear; no specific complaints about muddiness or dropouts. Reviewers note the audio sounds “perfect” compared to other versions.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers highlight a consistently strong 1972 set, describing the performances as sparkling and energetic throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as “primed” with excellent guitar tone.
